title: stillingia lineata var. obtusifolia
intro: Stillingia lineata var. obtusifolia, also known as Stillingia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its root and leaves. The health benefits associated with Stillingia include its potential in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Stillingia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional and folk medicine for its medicinal purposes.
